name: Montgó Natural Park Loop from Dénia
name_original: Parque Natural del Montgó – Circular desde Dénia hasta Jávea
description: Cycle 21.2 miles (34.1 km) through Parque Natural del Montgó from Dénia to Jávea, enjoying coastal views and the Cap de Sant Antoni Lighthou
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Dénia offer a diverse landscape for riders, characterized by the prominent Montgó Massif, a varied coastline, and fertile agricultural plains. The Montgó mountain rises from the sea, providing challenging ascents and descents through pine forests. The coastline features both sandy beaches and rocky coves, while inland areas consist of extensive orange and almond groves, offering flatter routes. Best touring cycling routes around Dénia The most popular touring cycling route is Montgó Natural Park Loop from Dénia, a 21.2 miles (34.1 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain within the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Dénia Greenway and Coastal Loop, an easy 12.6 miles (20.3 km) path. This route combines a flat greenway section with coastal views. Local touring cyclists also love the Dénia to Gandía Greenway – Dénia city centre loop from Bosc de Diana, a 32.1 miles (51.7 km) trail leading through agricultural plains, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Touring cycling around Dénia is defined by coastal paths, mountain trails within Montgó Natural Park, and routes through agricultural plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Dénia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1000 reviews. More than 9000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Dénia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Dénia?
A: Dénia offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 200 options available. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy greenways to challenging mountain climbs, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Dénia?
A: The routes in Dénia are quite diverse. You'll find approximately 92 easy routes, 70 moderate routes, and 54 difficult routes. This variety means cyclists of all experience levels can find suitable paths, whether you're looking for a relaxed ride or a strenuous challenge.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Dénia?
A: The touring cycling experience in Dénia is highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 1000 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to mountain trails, and the well-maintained routes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Dénia?
A: Dénia's touring cycling routes showcase a diverse landscape. You'll encounter the majestic Montgó Massif, offering challenging ascents and descents through pine forests. The coastline features both fine sandy beaches and rocky coves, while inland, you'll cycle through fertile agricultural plains dotted with orange and almond groves.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or landmarks I can visit along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Dénia's routes often pass by or near significant natural attractions. You might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt;, a sea-level cave, or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2211732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgó Summit&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Salty Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5407844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly touring cycling routes in Dénia?
A: Absolutely. For an easy and family-friendly option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952628405/denia-greenway-and-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dénia Greenway and Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This 12.6-mile (20.3 km) path combines a flat greenway section, ideal for all ages, with pleasant coastal views. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31720808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dénia city centre – Via Verde de Dénia loop from Bosc de Diana&lt;/a&gt;, which also utilizes the flat former railway line.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes with significant elevation in Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia is an excellent destination for challenging rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951989821/montgo-natural-park-loop-from-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgó Natural Park Loop from Dénia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 21.2-mile (34.1 km) route with over 400 meters of elevation gain, taking you through the natural park's mountainous terrain. For an even more demanding experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39541711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario de Pla de Petracos – View of Vall d'Ebo loop from la Xara&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.4-mile (73 km) route with over 1000 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Dénia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951989821/montgo-natural-park-loop-from-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgó Natural Park Loop from Dénia&lt;/a&gt; and the easier &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952628405/denia-greenway-and-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dénia Greenway and Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt;. These circular options make planning your ride more convenient.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Dénia?
A: Dénia benefits from a pleasant, warm climate that allows for year-round outdoor activities, including touring cycling. While cycling is enjoyable throughout the year, the spring and autumn months offer particularly comfortable temperatures, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the cooler, though still mild, winter days.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass through agricultural areas?
A: Yes, Dénia's inland areas feature fertile plains. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31466583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dénia to Gandía Greenway – Dénia city centre loop from Bosc de Diana&lt;/a&gt; take you through extensive orange and almond groves, offering a picturesque glimpse into traditional Spanish rural life and generally flatter terrain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Dénia?
A: While many natural areas in Dénia are dog-friendly for walking, specific regulations for cycling with dogs can vary. It's always best to check local signage or the specific route details on komoot for any restrictions regarding dogs, especially within protected areas like the Montgó Natural Park, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that offer scenic coastal views?
A: Dénia's nearly 20 kilometers of coastline provide ample opportunities for scenic coastal rides. The region features routes that explore both the sandy beaches to the north and the rocky coves of Les Rotes to the south. The Cap de Sant Antoni, with its lighthouse, is a particularly popular spot for spectacular coastal vistas.
tips: Picturesque path following the course of the River Gorgos along the slopes of Montgó with very interesting views of the entire Marina Alta valley.

The lighthouse that overlooks the bay from Cap de Sant Antoni was the successor to other defensive structures that took advantage of the privileged location of the plain. Hermits built the chapel of San Antonio in the 14th century to isolate themselves from secular life. It was built in his honor and eventually gave the relief its name. A watchtower to protect against pirate raids was also built on this ground, which later gave way to the current lighthouse. This viewpoint is without a doubt one of the most privileged locations on the Valencian coast.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Based on Komoot's data, this touring bicycle route typically takes around 2 hours and 11 minutes to complete. This is for the full 34 km circular route from Dénia to Jávea.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, starting and ending in Dénia. You can begin from various points within Dénia, such as near the coast or the town center, as it loops through the area and towards Jávea. Parking is generally available in Dénia, but specific trailhead parking for this exact route isn't explicitly designated as it's a town-to-town circular ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily designed for touring bicycles, featuring mostly paved surfaces. You'll find about 21.3 km of asphalt and 12.5 km of other paved sections. There are only minimal unpaved sections (around 214 meters) and less than 100 meters of cobblestones, making it very rideable for touring cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered 'moderate' in difficulty, requiring good fitness due to its distance and elevation gain. While it's mostly paved, the Montgó Natural Park does have varying terrain. If you're a beginner, ensure you have a comfortable touring bike and are prepared for the distance and some climbs.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse range of landscapes, from panoramic coastal views of Les Rotes, Dénia, and the Xàbia Bay, to the impressive Montgó massif. The route crosses Les Planes, with its scrubland, woods, and old terraced farm plots. On clear days, you might even spot Ibiza or Mallorca in the distance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for cycling. Summer can be very hot, especially in the middle of the day, and winter might bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the Pòpul Hermitage, cycle along the Gorgos River Trail, and reach the stunning Cap de Sant Antoni Lighthouse, which offers fantastic coastal views. You can also see 'Els Molins' (windmills) from Bahía de Xàbia and enjoy a scenic viewpoint near the lighthouse.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of 'El Camí IT5TR1 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana' (21% of the route) and 'Camino del Alba' (13%). You'll also encounter smaller segments of paths like 'Xàbia - Montgo' and 'Avinguda del Marquesat'.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the Parque Natural del Montgó generally allows dogs, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas and near wildlife. Given this is a touring bicycle route, ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling alongside or in a trailer, and always respect local regulations regarding pets in natural parks.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for Parque Natural del Montgó?
faqs.answer: No, there is no entrance fee or specific permit required to cycle this route through the Parque Natural del Montgó. The park is freely accessible to the public. However, always respect the natural environment and follow any posted regulations regarding cycling, waste, and wildlife.

faqs.question: What unique natural features can I see in Parque Natural del Montgó?
faqs.answer: The Montgó Natural Park is renowned for its exceptional biodiversity, housing over 650 plant species (many rare or endemic) and over 150 vertebrate species, including the Bonelli's eagle. The massif itself is a striking rocky mountain rising from the sea, creating diverse habitats. You'll also see ancient terraced farm plots, showcasing the region's agricultural history.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route connecting Dénia and Jávea, both towns offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel. Along the route itself, especially closer to the towns or popular viewpoints, you might find some establishments, but it's always wise to carry water and snacks, particularly for the sections within the natural park.
